findings.message: plugin.json has no skills array; the plugin's sole artifact skills/adversarial-spec/SKILL.md is not declared in the manifest, breaking auto-discovery
skills/adversarial-spec/SKILL.md is the plugin's sole NL artifact but is absent from any skills[] declaration in plugin.json; tooling that relies on explicit manifest declarations cannot locate it
litellm==1.80.13 is exact-pinned to one version; security patches in later patch releases are missed until the pin is manually updated
Zero example blocks present; skills require at least one worked invocation example showing input and expected output
'comprehensive' used twice as a vague quantifier; replace with concrete criteria such as 'covers all 8 interview topic categories' or 'all required sections populated'
'thorough' is a vague quantifier; replace with 'covers all 8 interview topic categories in Step 0.5'
'suitable' is a vague quantifier; specify which import format is produced (e.g., 'JSON compatible with Jira/Linear import schemas')

Bash command template at Step 3 interpolates MODEL_LIST (derived from AskUserQuestion, which allows free-text 'Other' input) without quoting; a value like 'gpt-4o; cat ~/.ssh/id_rsa' would be executed by the shell

subprocess.run at line 330 passes user-provided model name as a positional CLI argument to external 'codex' binary without validating the model name format; a crafted model string could inject unexpected flags
Plugin writes config and profile data to user home directory locations (~/.claude/adversarial-spec/ and ~/.config/adversarial-spec/) that are outside the repo and undocumented in the SKILL.md
Full spec content transmitted to external Telegram API on every debate round; TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN from environment; no explicit user acknowledgment of data sharing required before --telegram is used
plugin.json has no skills array; the plugin's sole artifact skills/adversarial-spec/SKILL.md is not declared in the manifest, breaking auto-discovery

Zero example blocks present; skills require at least one worked invocation example showing input and expected output — No example blocks found anywhere in SKILL.md (searched for ## Examples, <example>, and similar markers)

'comprehensive' used twice as a vague quantifier; replace with concrete criteria such as 'covers all 8 interview topic categories' or 'all required sections populated' — "conduct a comprehensive interview" (line ~200) and "write a comprehensive spec" (line ~263)
'thorough' is a vague quantifier; replace with 'covers all 8 interview topic categories in Step 0.5' — "it's a thorough requirements gathering session" at line ~200
'suitable' is a vague quantifier; specify which import format is produced (e.g., 'JSON compatible with Jira/Linear import schemas') — "suitable for importing into issue trackers" at line ~839

Bash command template at Step 3 interpolates MODEL_LIST (derived from AskUserQuestion, which allows free-text 'Other' input) without quoting; a value like 'gpt-4o; cat ~/.ssh/id_rsa' would be executed by the shell

Full spec content transmitted to external Telegram API on every debate round; TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN from environment; no explicit user acknowledgment of data sharing required before --telegram is used — TELEGRAM_API.format(token=token, method=method) at line 63 sends full spec content to api.telegram.org on every debate round via send_long_message

subprocess.run at line 330 passes user-provided model name as a positional CLI argument to external 'codex' binary without validating the model name format; a crafted model string could inject unexpected flags

Plugin writes config and profile data to user home directory locations (~/.claude/adversarial-spec/ and ~/.config/adversarial-spec/) that are outside the repo and undocumented in the SKILL.md — GLOBAL_CONFIG_PATH = Path.home() / ".claude" / "adversarial-spec" / "config.json" at line 15; save_global_config writes to this path at line 101
litellm==1.80.13 is exact-pinned to one version; security patches in later patch releases are missed until the pin is manually updated
skills/adversarial-spec/SKILL.md is the plugin's sole NL artifact but is absent from any skills[] declaration in plugin.json; tooling that relies on explicit manifest declarations cannot locate it
